ELR Name: BALHAM JN - BECKENHAM JN
Free Text:
Construction of a new under track crossing (UTX) using horizontal directional drilling technique (HDD) to install a new electricity cable route beneath railway tracks, at engineer’s line reference: BBJ; mileages: 10m 59ch
The scope involves the installation of two new ducts, each 558mm in diameter and spaced a minimum of 2.5m apart, beneath both Network Rail and TfL Tram tracks, between Avenue Road and Villiers Road, Beckenham, BR3 4SA. These ducts will be installed using HDD, maintaining a vertical separation of more than 8m below the tracks. Please note that the new UTX will be owned and maintained by UKPN

ELR Name: REDHILL TONBRIDGE LINE JN - TONBRIDGE WEST JN
Free Text: Works are to provide a defined GRP walking route which clearly demarcates where depot staff are to walk. The walkway has been set to 1.3m offset from running face of the nearest running rail.  As a minumim, this provides a 50mm build tolerance prior to clearances becoming non-compliant. In accordance with standard RIS-3413-TOM, limited clearance signs are to be provided where a positon of safety is interrupted for a distance of more than 2m. Where 1.3m clearance a minimum of 750mm will be allowable due to the site being within a depot. The height of the walkway has been proposed to be raised 150mm above the adjacent ground level.

Within Network Rail's Mining Referral area. 
Site is located in an area of known historic or current mining, and surface defects associated with mining may be present or develop over time.
If planned work includes anything which could be deemed 'qualifying construction work' under NR/L2/CIV/191/Mod 5, a Stage 1 Mining Report should be requested from Technical Authority’s Mining Team Connect page:
